sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, event_time DATETIME, description VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, event_time, description) VALUES (1, curdatetime(100), 'Initial event');
INSERT INTO test_table (id, event_time, description) VALUES (2, curdatetime(200), 'Second event');
SELECT id, event_time, description FROM test_table WHERE isLiteral(description) AND event_time < curdatetime(150);
SELECT id, COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT id, event_time, description FROM test_table WHERE isLiteral(description)) AS subquery GROUP BY id HAVING COUNT(*) > 0;
SELECT id, curdatetime((SELECT AVG(id) FROM test_table)) AS adjusted_time FROM test_table WHERE event_time IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE another_table (related_id INT, value_text VARCHAR(100), value_date DATETIME, FOREIGN KEY (related_id) REFERENCES test_table(id));
INSERT INTO another_table (related_id, value_text, value_date) VALUES (1, 'Related entry', curdatetime(50));
SELECT at.related_id, at.value_text, curdatetime(0) FROM another_table at INNER JOIN test_table tt ON isLiteral(at.value_text) AND at.related_id = tt.id WHERE at.value_date > curdatetime(500);

SELECT tt.id, at.value_text, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM another_table WHERE related_id = tt.id AND value_date IS NOT NULL) AS related_count FROM test_table tt LEFT JOIN another_table at ON at.related_id = tt.id WHERE at.value_text IS NOT NULL GROUP BY tt.id HAVING related_count > 0;
